event_blocks.summary: An orchestral score accompanies an aerial view of Dubai’s Palm Jumeirah and the Atlantis The Palm hotel, transitioning to a cityscape featuring the Burj Khalifa under a golden sky, before shifting to an indoor awards ceremony. Tom Urquhart, in a tuxedo, stands at a golden podium on a stage with a dark blue, geometrically patterned backdrop illuminated by orange and white lights, identified by a lower-third graphic as part of the 'Nakheel Dubai Globe Soccer Awards'. He welcomes 'distinguished guests, ladies and gentlemen, football fans' to the '18th Annual Dubai International Sports Conference' and the '14th edition of the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards', organized annually by the Dubai Sports Council since 2006. Urquhart, using expressive hand gestures, discusses the 'Sports Conference,' highlighting 'substantial debates' and 'discussions around topics' exploring football as a 'global phenomenon,' with a diverse, formally dressed audience visible in white armchairs. He emphasizes the evening's theme of 'sustainability and performance in football,' stating the event recognizes the 'extraordinary importance of environmental responsibility' and commits to measuring and offsetting emissions for a 'carbon neutral event in the future.' Upbeat background music underscores the atmosphere, with a wider shot revealing a second podium and a screen displaying 'Dubai International Sports Conference — 18th Edition' and sponsor logos, reinforcing the focus on 'SUSTAINABILITY & PERFORMANCE IN FOOTBALL.'

Tom Urquhart.description: A man in a black tuxedo with a bow tie, standing at a golden podium, speaking into a microphone with expressive hand gestures. He speaks with a clear British accent, discussing the event's focus on substantial debates, topics exploring football as a global phenomenon, and the central theme of sustainability and performance in football. He emphasizes the Dubai Sports Council and Dubai Globe Soccer Awards' commitment to environmental responsibility, measuring emissions, and achieving a carbon-neutral event in the future.

Palm Jumeirah.description: An artificial archipelago shaped like a palm tree, featuring numerous finger-like islands with luxury residences, connected by bridges and surrounded by deep blue waters.

Atlantis The Palm Hotel.description: A large, ornate, beige-colored resort with multiple towers and turrets, located at the base of the Palm Jumeirah, visible in aerial shots. It is explicitly announced by a female voice as the live location for the event.

Dubai Cityscape.description: A dense skyline of modern skyscrapers, including the Burj Khalifa, visible in the background of aerial shots, bathed in warm sunlight. The city is the host for the '18th Annual Dubai International Sports Conference' and the '14th edition of the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards', as confirmed by the speakers.

Nakheel Dubai Globe Soccer Awards.description: An event identified by on-screen graphics, featuring a dark blue stage with geometric patterns and dynamic orange lighting, where Tom Urquhart is presenting. The stage includes a large screen displaying 'Dubai International Sports Conference — 18th Edition' and sponsor logos such as Dubai Sports Council and Nakheel, with a theme of 'SUSTAINABILITY & PERFORMANCE IN FOOTBALL.' The speaker explicitly states the event, hosted by the Dubai Sports Council and Dubai Globe Soccer Awards, is committed to environmental responsibility, measuring and offsetting emissions, and aiming for a carbon-neutral future.

Stage Backdrop.description: A dynamic, dark blue background with glowing orange hexagonal patterns and sparkling lights that pulse rhythmically, creating a futuristic and high-tech ambiance. The backdrop includes a large screen displaying event branding and sponsor logos.

Second Podium.description: A secondary, identical golden podium positioned on the left side of the stage, currently unoccupied, suggesting a co-host or panelist may join later.

Audience Members.description: A diverse group of formally dressed attendees seated in white armchairs, including men in traditional Middle Eastern attire (such as keffiyehs) and Western suits, and women in elegant evening gowns. They are seated at small tables with bottled water, snacks, and decorative items, showing varying levels of engagement with the speaker.

event_blocks.summary: The Nakheel Dubai Globe Soccer Awards commenced on a stage featuring a dark blue backdrop with glowing orange hexagonal patterns and dynamic lighting, as Tom Urquhart, in a tuxedo, welcomed the audience. Sports anchor Amanda Davies, in a strapless black sequined gown and diamond jewelry, then took the stage, introducing Manchester City's Ruben Dias and subsequently Kyle Walker, who ascended a ramp past a golden soccer ball sculpture to join a panel. The event transitioned to a conversation with Cristiano Ronaldo, who, after being introduced by Davies as a legendary goal scorer, walked confidently to a black chair on stage, joining a second panelist. Davies then questioned Ronaldo about Manchester City's success, to which he responded with congratulations, calling their season 'amazing' and acknowledging their 'very good team, very good coach,' and 'amazing' system, while also stating they 'should deserve to win maybe two years ago.'

She speaks into a lapel microphone, gesturing confidently during the panel discussion, her voice clear and articulate as she questions Cristiano Ronaldo about his football allegiances and appreciation for rival teams.

event_blocks.summary: At the Dubai International Sports Conference — 18th Edition, held at the Nakheel Dubai Globe Soccer Awards, Cristiano Ronaldo, clad in a dark blue suit over a black turtleneck, discussed his move to Saudi Arabia, stating, "I thought will be a good move to me. Why not to give a chance to another countries with my football, with my passion, to change mentalities." He elaborated that the Saudi league is "more competitive" and "better than France League," attributing improvements to "powerful people behind to help to change a little bit the culture of football, the bad habits," and "huge" investment, predicting it will reach the top three or four globally "step by step." Ronaldo also addressed criticism, noting, "I like when the people doubt about me and I prove again that... one year with successful." He highlighted his personal success, stating, "this year I did a fantastic season... I was the the best goal scorer. Imagine to beat that animals, the young lions like Allan for example." Moderator Amanda Davies, in a strapless black sequined gown, engaged with Ronaldo, Ruben Dias, and Kyle Walker, who also shared insights. Dias spoke about the collective effort required to win, stating, "it's a process, it's a team game and that's what we're in it for," and acknowledging managers and players. Walker discussed Manchester City's consistency, crediting the manager as a "massive driving force." The event featured a dynamic dark blue stage with glowing orange hexagonal patterns and sponsor logos, under vibrant, futuristic lighting, with an attentive audience, including Erling Haaland, observing the discussions.

raw_segments: The video opens with Amanda Davies, elegantly dressed in a sparkling strapless black gown and diamond necklace, standing at a golden podium adorned with the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo. As dynamic orange and yellow light patterns pulse on the dark stage backdrop, she speaks into a lapel microphone, hands clasped, stating, "There emerge superstars a plenty, looking at individual performances, team contributions and efforts across both domestic clubs and national teams. We answer the question now of just who is the best women's footballer in the world." The scene then transitions to Tom Urquhart, in a black tuxedo and bow tie, who takes the podium, adjusts his glasses, and gestures expressively while announcing, "Indeed. So let's move on to your finalists for the Nakheel Dubai Globe Soccer Awards Best Women's Player. And the finalists are..." The stage continues to glow with hexagonal patterns and sparkling effects. An animated golden graphic then appears, announcing the 'BEST WOMEN'S PLAYER' award, sponsored by 'إسعاد esaaD', with shimmering light trails and floating orbs. Uplifting music plays as Tom Urquhart introduces each nominee, accompanied by applause: Aitana Bonmatí, shown in a Barcelona jersey; Olga Carmona, in a Real Madrid jersey; Asisat Oshoala, in a Barcelona jersey; Linda Caicedo, in a Real Madrid jersey; Amanda Ilestedt, in an Arsenal jersey; and finally, Sam Kerr, in a Chelsea jersey, whose image lingers on screen as the presentation concludes.

The video opens with a golden animated graphic for the 'Best Women's Player' award, featuring shimmering light trails and the 'إسعاد esaaD' logo, accompanied by upbeat, celebratory music. The scene transitions to Amanda Davies, elegantly dressed in a strapless black gown with subtle sparkles and a diamond necklace, standing at the golden podium of the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards 2023. Speaking into a lapel microphone with a clear voice, she announces, "All very deserving winners... but there is one who has without doubt dominated in the last 12 months. This award has been sponsored by Esad." As dynamic orange hexagonal patterns pulse behind her on the stage backdrop, the camera cuts to the formally dressed audience, including men in traditional white keffiyehs and women in gowns, who are seen clapping and taking photos, their enthusiastic applause and cheers audible. The focus returns to Amanda Davies as she dramatically declares, "And the winner is... Aitana Bonmatí from Spain and FC Barcelona," followed by more enthusiastic applause. She then introduces, "So let's hear from the Best Women's Player at the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards. Here is Aitana Bonmatí." The scene then shifts to an outdoor setting, where Aitana Bonmatí, wearing a blue Barcelona training jacket over a red shirt, stands in front of green foliage, speaking directly to the camera in Spanish. On-screen text identifies her as 'Aitana Bonmatí Best Women's Player of the Year,' as she expresses gratitude for the award, mentioning her club commitments and the Supercopa in Madrid, concluding her acceptance message in the serene outdoor environment.

event_blocks.summary: Aitana Bonmatí, wearing a blue Barcelona training jacket over a red shirt, spoke calmly in Spanish, expressing deep gratitude for a successful year, emphasizing the collective triumphs of the Champions League with Barça and the World Cup with the national team, calling it a unique and complete year. She dedicated her awards to teammates, staff, club workers, directors, and national team members, concluding with a hug and well wishes for the gala. The scene shifted to the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards, revealing a wide shot of the formally dressed audience, including men in white keffiyehs and women in elegant gowns, seated in white armchairs and audibly clapping as dynamic orange and yellow lights illuminated the stage. Amanda Davies, in a sparkling black gown and diamond necklace, stood at the golden podium, which featured the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo. Speaking professionally with expressive hand gestures, she explained Aitana's absence due to a Supercopa match against Madrid, relaying Aitana's gratitude and her belief that individual honors stem from teamwork, echoing Aitana's sentiment about owing achievements to teammates and staff. Tom Urquhart, in a black tuxedo, then took the golden podium, speaking seriously and professionally, adjusting his glasses and gesturing. He congratulated the initial winners and thanked Aitana for her remarks, all set against a backdrop of glowing hexagonal patterns and sparkling lights, confirming the 'Dubai Globe Soccer Awards 2023' with the Nakheel logo.

event_blocks.summary: The Dubai Globe Soccer Awards, presented by NAKHEEL, are expanding globally, with Tom Urquhart announcing the "inaugural event of our very first intercontinental series" will be held in Europe, specifically at the Cala di Volpe Hotel in Costa Smeralda, Sardinia, in May 2024, marking the first European edition after over a decade in Dubai. The event, held in a grand, dimly lit hall with a dynamic stage backdrop of glowing orange hexagonal patterns and sparkling lights, featured presenters Amanda Davies and Tom Urquhart. They introduced His Highness Sheikh Mansour bin Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum and His Excellency Matar Al Tayer to the stage to present awards. The 'Best Middle East Club' award was presented to Egyptian giants Al Ahly, with Mahmoud El Khatib accepting the golden, globe-shaped trophy. A presenter in a dark suit, speaking in formal Arabic, expressed gratitude to the UAE and acknowledged the "legends of football" present, dedicating the award to Al Ahly fans and a colleague facing a health crisis, amidst a celebratory orchestral score and vibrant lighting.

raw_segments: The video opens with Tom Urquhart, dressed in a black tuxedo with a bow tie, standing at a golden podium emblazoned with the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo. He speaks into a lapel microphone, gesturing with his hands and adjusting his glasses, against a dynamic stage backdrop of glowing orange hexagonal patterns and sparkling lights. Tom announces, with a teasing tone, that there's an "announcement" and "a little bit of a surprise," revealing that the "Dubai Globe Soccer Awards are truly, by name and by nature, going global this year." He explains that the football world is bracing itself for "the inaugural event of our very first intercontinental series," and asks the audience if they have any ideas for the "first stop," hinting it's in Europe. The scene transitions to a wide shot of the dark event venue with geometric designs and sponsor logos, including 'NAKHEEL' and 'Dubai Globe Soccer Awards'. A stylized world map animation then highlights Dubai in orange, followed by a transition to Cala di Volpe, Costa Smeralda, marked with a white triangle, as dramatic, building music begins to play. Aerial footage follows, showcasing the scenic coastal town with its marina, white buildings, and lush green hills. A voiceover then confirms that "After more than 10 years in Dubai, the Globe Soccer Awards head to the stunning Costa Smeralda region in Sardinia for the first stop of the Intercontinental Series." The camera pans over a luxurious resort with a large turquoise pool, surrounded by trees and a winding path, emphasizing the opulent setting, as the voiceover concludes that "The inaugural European edition will be held at the iconic Cala di Volpe Hotel in May 2024."

The video opens with an aerial view of a luxurious resort nestled between green hills and the sea, featuring terracotta-roofed buildings and a large turquoise pool, establishing a scenic backdrop. The scene transitions to a grand indoor event, the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards, where an upbeat, orchestral background score plays. A formally dressed audience sits in white armchairs, clapping and observing the stage. Tom Urquhart, a man in a black tuxedo with a bow tie, stands at a golden podium with the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo. He speaks with a clear, professional tone, announcing that this is where "the best European football talent will be rewarded for their outstanding achievements." He then mentions "Sardinia, the end of May," suggesting it "sounds like a good idea," to which Amanda Davies, a woman in a strapless black gown standing at the second golden podium, enthusiastically responds, "I'm in." Tom Urquhart encourages viewers to "do join us there" before declaring, "Right, on with the awards." Under dynamic orange and yellow lighting, with a stage backdrop featuring glowing hexagonal patterns and sponsor logos, he welcomes "His Highness, Sheikh Mansour bin Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum, the Chairman of the Dubai Sports Council," and "His Excellency, Matar Al Tayer, the Vice Chairman of the Dubai Sports Council," to the stage to present the awards. As he makes these introductions, two men in traditional white keffiyehs and long robes walk onto the stage, joining the host, while the audience erupts in applause, accompanied by a swelling, celebratory musical score. On-screen text announces the 'GLOBESOCCER AWARDS EUROPEAN EDITION' for 28 & 29 May 2024, reinforcing the event's prestige and global reach.

The segment opens with an upbeat, celebratory orchestral score as a wide shot reveals the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards stage, dynamically lit with orange and yellow patterns against a dark backdrop. Two golden podiums stand center, occupied by presenters Amanda Davies, in a sparkling black gown, and Tom Urquhart, in a black tuxedo, flanked by two men in traditional Emirati attire. The backdrop prominently displays 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' and sponsor logos. The formally dressed audience, some clapping, others observing, fills the foreground. The camera zooms to Amanda Davies, who, speaking into her lapel microphone with a clear, professional British accent, welcomes guests and highlights how football in the Middle East has "really captured the imagination," emphasizing the "consistency and ultimately excellence" required to honor the best club. The scene then transitions to Tom Urquhart at his podium, who, also speaking with a professional British accent, announces that the award is "generously sponsored by our friends at Tata 2" before introducing the finalists for the 'BEST MIDDLE EAST CLUB' category. As an animated graphic sequence with sparkling golden backgrounds and glowing hexagonal patterns displays the club logos, Tom Urquhart announces "Al Ahly," followed by "Al-Nassr," and finally "Al-Hilal," each announcement met with enthusiastic applause from the audience as the celebratory music continues to swell. Amanda Davies then begins to speak again, saying "And..." as the minute concludes.

The video opens in a grand, dimly lit event hall, where formally dressed attendees, including men in traditional white keffiyehs and women in elegant gowns, are seated in rows of white armchairs. On a sleek, modern stage with a dynamic backdrop of glowing orange hexagonal patterns and sparkling lights displaying sponsor logos, Amanda Davies, in a strapless black gown and diamond necklace, stands at a golden podium. On stage, a man in a dark suit presents a large, golden, globe-shaped trophy to Mahmoud El Khatib, the man in the green thobe, who accepts it with a handshake, while a man in a white thobe observes. Tom Urquhart then addresses Mahmoud, saying, "Mahmoud, you'd like to say a few words. Please head on over. I understand you want to say a few words," as the applause persists.

event_blocks.summary: The Dubai Globe Soccer Awards commenced with a wide shot of a stage featuring a backdrop of glowing orange hexagonal patterns and sponsor logos, with three golden podiums. Tom Urquhart, in a black tuxedo, stood at the central podium, congratulating "President Matt" and "Al-Khelaifi and all the team," and discussing the taxing yet rewarding role of an agent in facilitating transfers, negotiating contracts, and securing sponsorship. Amanda Davies, in a strapless black gown, then explained how agents manage all aspects of a footballer's career, allowing players to focus on the pitch. Following this, an "BEST AGENT" award graphic appeared, and nominees Ali Barat and Jorge Mendes were announced. The ceremony then highlighted Frank Trimboli with a golden animated graphic before Tom Urquhart announced Jorge Mendes as the winner. Mendes, in a dark suit, accepted the 'Best Agent of the Year' award, a golden globe-shaped trophy, from a presenter in a green thobe. Mendes delivered a speech thanking Globe Soccer, expressing pride in Gestifute's past year, and outlining ambitions for continued improvement through hard work, ambition, and passion, also thanking his wife, family, team, managers, and players. The event then shifted focus to the "master strategist" and "sporting director" award, with Tom Urquhart and Amanda Davies introducing the nominees: Txiki Begiristain, Cristiano Giuntoli, and Thiago Scuro. Amanda Davies announced Cristiano Giuntoli as the "Best Sporting Director," generously sponsored by Emirates. Giuntoli, in a black suit, walked to the stage as a narrator detailed his achievements with Napoli, including their Scudetto win and talent acquisition, and his subsequent move to Juventus. On stage, Giuntoli received the "Sporting Director of the Year" trophy from two men in traditional Emirati attire. He then moved to a podium to give his acceptance speech, thanking Napoli's president Aurelio De Laurentiis, coach Luciano Spalletti, players, and his family for their support during their Scudetto win, and outlining his objectives with Juventus to reach the Champions League and win the Coppa Italia, expressing hope for future awards with his new club.

raw_segments: The Dubai Globe Soccer Awards stage opens with a wide shot, showcasing a dynamic backdrop of glowing orange hexagonal patterns and sponsor logos like 'NAKHEEL'. Three golden podiums are set, with Tom Urquhart, in a black tuxedo, at the central one. Amidst applause, Tom speaks into his lapel microphone, gesturing and adjusting his glasses, offering congratulations to "President Matt" and "Al-Khelaifi and all the team." He elaborates on the agent's role, from "facilitating transfers" to "negotiating contracts" and "securing sponsorship," describing it as "extremely rewarding but also incredibly taxing." The camera then cuts to Amanda Davies, elegant in a strapless black gown, at the left podium. Her voice takes over, explaining how agents "look after every aspect of a footballer's career," allowing players to "focus on the job at hand on the pitch." Tom's voice returns, further emphasizing "how crucial a role the agent plays" and stating they are there "to pay tribute to those often unsung heroes." Amanda then announces, "Your nominees are...", as the scene transitions to a shimmering 'BEST AGENT' award graphic against a starry, golden background. Dramatic music swells as she names the first nominee, "Ali Barat," whose image appears in a vertical frame, followed by "Jorge Mendes," also shown in a vertical frame, before the audio cuts off.

The video opens with a golden animated graphic announcing the 'BEST AGENT' award, featuring a portrait of Frank Trimboli in a dark suit, whose name is audibly announced by Amanda Davies, alongside 'DUBAI' logos and shimmering light effects. Tom Urquhart, dressed in a black tuxedo with a bow tie, stands at a golden podium labeled 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS', speaking into a lapel microphone, announcing a sponsor and then building anticipation for the 'Best Agent' award. The camera pans to the formally dressed audience, including men in traditional white keffiyehs and women in elegant gowns, seated in white armchairs, some capturing the moment on their phones. Tom Urquhart then announces "Jorge Mendes" as the winner, prompting enthusiastic applause and celebratory music. Jorge Mendes, in a dark suit, rises from the audience and walks toward the stage, where he is greeted by a man in a green thobe and white keffiyeh holding a golden globe-shaped trophy. Mendes accepts the 'Best Agent of the Year' award, smiling as he stands beside the presenter, with his portrait displayed on a large screen against a backdrop of glowing orange patterns and sponsor logos. The scene concludes with a wide shot of the full stage, revealing Amanda Davies in a strapless black gown at one golden podium and Tom Urquhart at the other, while Amanda Davies speaks, congratulating Mendes and highlighting his agency's achievements and clients like Enzo Fernandez and Gonzalo Ramos.

The segment opens on a dimly lit stage where Jorge Mendes, dressed in a dark suit, stands seriously at a golden podium, speaking into a handheld microphone. Behind him, his name 'JORGE MENDES' is displayed on a large screen, illuminated by dynamic orange and yellow light streaks and glowing hexagonal patterns. He delivers a heartfelt speech in English, thanking Globe Soccer for the award, expressing deep pride in his company Gestifute's successful past year, and outlining his ambition for continued improvement through hard work, ambition, and passion. He extends his gratitude to his wife, family, Gestifute team, managers, and players, concluding with a 'Happy New Year' to all. As he finishes, the grand, dimly lit venue, adorned with elegant architectural details and sponsor logos like 'NAKHEEL' and 'Dubai Globe Soccer Awards', fills with enthusiastic applause and cheers from the formally dressed audience, many in white keffiyehs, some capturing the moment on their phones. The scene then transitions back to the stage, where Tom Urquhart, in a black tuxedo, takes over the golden podium, now speaking into a lapel microphone. He gestures expressively, congratulating Jorge Mendes and announcing the transition to the 'master strategist' for the next award, the individual responsible for football operations, as the vibrant stage lights continue to pulse.

The segment opens with Tom Urquhart, a man in a black tuxedo with a bow tie, standing at the golden podium of the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards. Against a dynamic backdrop of pulsing orange and yellow geometric light patterns, he speaks into a lapel microphone, gesturing with his hands, stating, "It's now time for us to pay tribute to the sporting directors." Amanda Davies, in a sparkling strapless black gown and diamond necklace, then takes over at the same podium, speaking with expressive hand gestures about the role: "Their duties may vary, but ultimately the sporting director develops and protects the overall vision and philosophy... a large part of a club's success falls on the sporting director." Tom Urquhart interjects, "Doesn't it just? Let's have a look at your finalists in this category and they are..." as the scene transitions to a graphic announcing the 'BEST SPORTING DIRECTOR' award. Set against a dark background with shimmering golden lights and Emirates logos, the graphic features nominees Txiki Begiristain (Manchester City), Cristiano Giuntoli (Napoli), and Thiago Scuro (Monaco), whose names are announced by Tom Urquhart over upbeat, celebratory music. The camera then pulls back to a wide shot of the formally dressed audience seated in white armchairs, some capturing the event on their phones, with the stage illuminated by golden light trails. Amanda Davies returns to the podium, continuing her presentation, and dramatically announces, "Generously sponsored by Emirates, your Nakheel Dubai Globe Soccer Awards Best Sporting Director is... Cristiano Giuntoli!" As the audience applauds, a man in a black suit, identified as Cristiano Giuntoli, stands up from his seat and walks down the aisle towards the stage, maintaining the formal and celebratory atmosphere with consistent event branding.

The segment opens at the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards with upbeat, celebratory music playing as Cristiano Giuntoli, a man in a black suit, walks up the stairs to a stage illuminated with dynamic orange and yellow geometric light patterns and event branding. A male narrator's voice overlays the music, detailing Giuntoli's achievements, including Napoli's Scudetto win and his role in talent acquisition and collaboration with coach Luciano Spalletti, also mentioning his subsequent move to Juventus. On stage, Giuntoli is greeted by two men in traditional Emirati attire; the one in a green thobe presents him with a large, golden, globe-shaped 'Sporting Director of the Year' trophy. Giuntoli accepts with a smile, shakes hands, and stands between the two men holding his award. With his name 'CRISTIAN GIUNTOLI' displayed in large white letters behind him, he begins his acceptance speech, speaking with a clear Italian accent, stating, "Thank you. I... I am honored to be here tonight. And uh, for me, '23 is very important year, historical year. And uh..." as he gestures and occasionally looks at notes, with a bottle of water beside him. The vibrant orange lights of the stage backdrop and sponsor logos like 'NAKHEEL' and 'Audi' maintain the formal and celebratory atmosphere.

event_blocks.summary: At the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards, Tom Urquhart, in a black tuxedo, stood at the golden podium, thanking "Cristiano" and "the team" amidst applause before introducing the "Best Goalkeeper" award, highlighting the modern keeper's dual role as a "sweeper keeper to supreme stopper" and their "blinding performances." Amanda Davies, in a sparkling black gown, announced the award, sponsored by "Sushi Sambao," as a graphic sequence showcased nominees Yassine Bounou, Ederson, and Thibaut Courtois, each met with audience applause. Later, Tom Urquhart declared Ederson the winner, prompting enthusiastic applause from the formally dressed audience, including Erling Haaland. A highlight reel detailed Ederson's "heroic role" in Manchester City's success, noting "19 clean sheets," "conceded only 30 goals in 35 Premier League games," and "conceded only 4 goals in 11 Champions League games," praising his "ball-playing ability" and "sweeper keeper and traditional stopper" skills, culminating in the "EDERSON BEST GOALKEEPER" announcement. Ederson, in a black suit, walked to the stage to receive the golden, globe-shaped trophy from a presenter in a green thobe, with on-screen text confirming him as 'Best Goalkeeper of the Year.' Amanda Davies interviewed Ederson, who, with a Brazilian accent, described working with Pep Guardiola as "so easy," stating, "he push the team up, he push the players," and noting his six-and-a-half years collecting trophies with him, all against a backdrop of sponsor logos and pulsing lights.

event_blocks.summary: At the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards, Amanda Davies, in a sparkling strapless black gown, interviewed Ederson, the best goalkeeper of the year, who held a golden globe-shaped trophy. The stage featured dynamic orange and yellow geometric light patterns and sponsor logos. Tom Urquhart took the golden podium, acknowledging another award for Manchester City and discussing the crucial role of midfielders. Amanda Davies then highlighted the evolving role of midfielders and players who raised the bar in 2023. Tom Urquhart announced the finalists for the Best Midfielder award: Jude Bellingham, Kevin De Bruyne, Rodrigo, and Bernardo Silva. Amanda Davies announced Rodri as the winner, sponsored by Power Horse, to enthusiastic applause. Narrators described Rodri as a player who lives for big moments and has cemented himself as one of the finest central defensive midfielders, highlighting his consistency and reliability. Rodri, in a light blue jersey, was shown scoring the Champions League winning goal against Inter Milan, a moment confirmed by on-screen text, with a voiceover noting his superb shooting ability, innate elegance, and role as a match-winner. The scene transitioned to a celebratory montage culminating in the 'RODRI BEST MIDFIELDER' announcement. Rodri, in a black suit, walked down the aisle to receive a golden globe-shaped trophy from a man in a green thobe. Amanda Davies interviewed Rodri, who reflected on the Champions League final, stating he told his teammates to keep pushing for the remaining 20 minutes and that scoring the winning goal was the most important moment of his career, which he had watched back perhaps 5,000 times. Bright orange flares enhanced the celebratory mood during this interaction.

Amanda Davies, in a sparkling strapless black gown and diamond necklace, interviews Rodri, who is in a sharp black suit, holding the large, globe-shaped Golden Trophy. Rodri, with a composed and reflective expression, speaks into the microphone held by Amanda, recalling a tense moment in a game: "I think it was like 20 minutes left... I didn't thought it was done... I was saying, guys, we have the 20 minutes of your lives, let's keep pushing." He describes the game's intensity, mentioning saves and near misses, before reflecting on the project's success and his immense pride: "I cannot be more proud of myself and my teammates. I think it was unbelievable season for us." Amanda, listening attentively and smiling, then asks, "How many times have you watched it back?" Rodri responds, "I said before, I think it's of course so far the most important moment of my career. Maybe 5,000 times, but always is the same." Bright orange flares sweep across the screen, enhancing the celebratory mood as this professional yet warm interaction captures Rodri's reflections on his award-winning season.

event_blocks.summary: At the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards, Amanda Davies, in a sparkling black gown, interviewed Rodrigo, who held a Golden Trophy and spoke of his pride and dreams of repeating success. Tom Urquhart, in a tuxedo, acknowledged the growth of women's club football, noting increased investment and success. Amanda Davies then introduced the 'Best Women's Club' category, announcing FC Barcelona, Chelsea, and Arsenal as finalists. Tom Urquhart declared FC Barcelona the winner, met with cheers and applause. Highlights of Barcelona's women's team followed, detailing their dominant season with 28 wins from 30 league games and 118 goals scored, including their second UEFA Women's Champions League title. A graphic announced 'BARCELONA BEST WOMEN'S CLUB' as the setting returned to the grand event venue. A man in a green thobe, identified as the director responsible for women's football at FC Barcelona, accepted the golden trophy from a presenter in a dark suit. The recipient, after accepting the award, stated, "Good evening." The stage displayed the FC Barcelona crest and 'BEST WOMEN'S CLUB'. The Presenter in Dark Suit then formally represented FC Barcelona, accepting the Best Women's Team of the Year award, attributing it to collective effort and the club's commitment to women's football. He also acknowledged Aitana Bonmatí's Best Player award, noting her absence due to focusing on a Super Cup final, and concluded by expressing immense honor and gratitude.

event_blocks.summary: The Dubai Globe Soccer Awards ceremony commenced with Cristiano Ronaldo, amidst formally dressed attendees, clapping as the scene shifted to the stage backdrop featuring 'NAKHEEL' and 'DUBAI SPORTS COUNCIL' illuminated by orange and yellow geometric lights. A Presenter in Dark Suit at the Golden Podium announced FC Barcelona's 'Best Women's Club' award, then introduced a segment honoring 'greats of the game.' Amanda Davies, in a black gown, joined him to present the 'career award generously sponsored by Serie A' to John Terry, the 'former England and Chelsea captain.' As the audience applauded, John Terry, the Winner in Black Suit, walked towards the stage, receiving a golden trophy from a Recipient in Green Thobe, who was accompanied by a Recipient in White Thobe. Later, John Terry, holding his 'John Terry Player Career Award,' spoke emotionally at a golden podium, dedicating his award to Gianluca Vialli and thanking Chelsea, the Dubai Sports Council, and Globe Soccer Awards, while acknowledging the impossibility of individual awards without teammates and managers. Amanda Davies interviewed Terry, asking about Vialli's advice, which he recalled as "Don't do what I do, do what I say," and playfully confirmed he was wearing his kit underneath his tuxedo. Terry then discussed Chelsea's current performance, contrasting it with Manchester City's dominance and expressing confidence in manager Pochettino and the young squad, despite acknowledging they were 'a long way off' top teams. The event continued with a presenter introducing the next award, sponsored by SilverSun, and Amanda Davies preparing to announce another career award winner amidst the dynamic, pulsing stage lighting.

event_blocks.summary: Amanda Davies, at the Nakheel Dubai Globe Soccer Awards golden podium, announced Lionel Scaloni as the World Cup-winning coach, as pulsing orange and yellow lights illuminated the stage. The audience, dressed formally and some holding phones, applauded loudly to a celebratory orchestral score. A montage narrated Scaloni's journey from 2018 to leading Argentina to the 2021 Copa America and the 2022 FIFA World Cup, highlighting his status as the fourth youngest coach to win the World Cup. Scaloni then walked the red carpet to receive the Coach Career Award from recipients in traditional attire. He began his acceptance speech in Spanish, dedicating the award to his country, people, and technical staff, including Pablo Aimar and Walter Samuel, describing their achievement as "like a movie." Cristiano Ronaldo was seen in the audience, appearing emotional. Scaloni thanked his family, wife, and children, expressing profound gratitude. Hosts Tom Urquhart and Amanda Davies then presented the Best Men's Club Award, with Davies noting the winner's seventh Premier League title and fifth in six seasons, and their undefeated UEFA Champions League win. Urquhart added their first UEFA Super Cup victory and Davies concluded with their FIFA Club World Cup debut win. Manchester City was announced as the Best Men's Club, met with enthusiastic applause. A highlight montage showcased their achievements, including being the first English side to win five major trophies in a calendar year, winning 28 of 38 Premier League games with a 73% win ratio, and their UEFA Champions League victory over Inter Milan. They were also noted as being undefeated in winning their first Champions League and FIFA Club World Cup titles, and the first English club to simultaneously hold the Premier League, FA Cup, UEFA Champions League, UEFA Super Cup, and FIFA Club World Cup.

raw_segments: The video opens with Amanda Davies, elegantly dressed in a strapless black gown with subtle sparkles and a diamond necklace, standing at the golden podium of the Nakheel Dubai Globe Soccer Awards. Her clear voice announces "World Cup winning coach, Lionel Scaloni," as the stage is illuminated by pulsing orange and yellow geometric light patterns. The scene cuts to the audience, where formally dressed attendees, including men in traditional white keffiyehs and women in gowns, are seated in white armchairs, some capturing the event on their phones, and applauding loudly. Upbeat, celebratory orchestral music begins as the narrative shifts to a dynamic montage celebrating Lionel Scaloni’s achievements, narrated by a male voice detailing his journey from taking over a struggling Argentina team in 2018 to helping them find a new identity. The narration highlights the team's flourishing success, including the 2021 Copa America victory that ended a 28-year drought, and their "date with destiny in Qatar" culminating in the 2022 FIFA World Cup win, making Argentina world champions for a third time and catapulting Scaloni to greatness. On-screen text further emphasizes his status as the fourth youngest coach to win the World Cup, accompanied by footage of Argentina players celebrating, Scaloni on the field, and the team disembarking from an airplane with medals. The video culminates with Scaloni walking down the red carpet pathway toward the stage, where he is presented with the golden Coach Career Award by two men in traditional attire—one in a green thobe and the other in a white thobe—against a backdrop of glowing lights and the event’s branding, with the celebratory music continuing as the audience applauds his reception of the trophy.

The segment opens with an upbeat, celebratory orchestral score as Lionel Scaloni, dressed in a black suit, receives the golden globe-shaped Coach Career Award from two men in traditional Emirati attire. He smiles, waves to the audience, and walks towards the center stage of the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards, where a large screen displays his name and event branding. A female voice announces, "So, congratulations. The mic is yours," as Scaloni approaches a golden circular podium. He begins his acceptance speech, first apologizing in English, "Hello, sorry for my English is very bad. I speak Spanish," before switching to Spanish. Gesturing with his hands and occasionally glancing at notes beside a bottle of water, he thanks for the award, dedicating it to his country and people, stating, "Honestly, what we've achieved has been like a movie, a film." He then dedicates it to "all Argentinians" and his technical staff, specifically naming Pablo Aimar, Walter Samuel, Roberto Ayala, Matías Manna, Martín Tocalli, Luis Martín, and Rodrigo Barrios, acknowledging their help in achieving "this incredible..." The audience, seated in white armchairs, watches attentively, some filming, while the stage backdrop pulses with orange and yellow geometric lights. A brief cutaway shows Cristiano Ronaldo in the audience, appearing emotional as he wipes his face, before returning to Scaloni, who continues his composed and reflective speech, maintaining eye contact, amidst the grand, celebratory atmosphere.

The video opens with Lionel Scaloni, dressed in a black suit, white shirt, and black tie, standing at a golden circular podium, delivering a speech in Spanish, thanking his family, wife, and children, and expressing profound gratitude, stating, "Thank you very much, that's all I have to say. Thank you, thank you." Behind him, a large screen displays his name against pulsing orange and yellow geometric light patterns. As he speaks, he gestures subtly and glances at notes, with a bottle of water beside him. The camera then cuts to a close-up of Cristiano Ronaldo in the audience, wearing a dark blue textured suit over a black turtleneck, appearing emotional as he wipes his face with his hand, a diamond earring and large ring visible. A wide shot reveals the formally dressed audience, seated in white armchairs, some capturing the event on their phones, erupting in applause after Scaloni's speech. The scene shifts to a grand stage with three golden podiums, where two hosts, Tom Urquhart in a black tuxedo and Amanda Davies in a strapless black gown, flank a central figure in a green thobe and white keffiyeh, who presents the golden, globe-shaped Coach Career Award to Lionel Scaloni. The stage backdrop features the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo, glowing geometric patterns, and sponsor logos. Tom Urquhart then takes the podium, speaking into a lapel microphone with expressive gestures, thanking the audience for their patience and those tuning in worldwide, before introducing the next award for "the finest club in the world," emphasizing the never-ending quest for balance and how "the good get separated from the great." Amanda Davies, holding a blue folder, then steps forward, lightheartedly asking if Scaloni persuaded Messi to stay for 2026, before engaging in conversation with Tom and beginning to announce the nominees for "the best men's..." all set against a backdrop of dramatic warm orange and yellow lighting and celebratory orchestral background music, as the audience continues to applaud.

The segment opens with a dynamic animated sequence for the 'BEST MEN'S CLUB' award, showcasing football club emblems against a dark, starry backdrop with glowing orange and yellow geometric light patterns. A female announcer's voice declares each club's name as its emblem appears, starting with "Manchester City," followed by "Al Ahly," "Barcelona," "Arsenal," "Napoli," and finally, "And Sevilla," each announcement met with crowd applause and celebratory background music. After the nominees are presented, the scene transitions to a live stage where Tom Urquhart, dressed in a black tuxedo, stands at a golden podium with the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo, speaking into a lapel microphone and gesturing. His clear male voice announces, "Right. Now. The award for the best men's club at this year's Nakheel Dubai Globe Soccer Awards are..." Amanda Davies, in a sparkling strapless black gown and diamond necklace, then takes to her golden podium, holding a blue folder, and adds with her clear female voice, "Time for something a little bit special, I think. The team who won the Premier League for a seventh time, fifth in the last six seasons." A wide shot reveals both Tom and Amanda on stage, with a large screen behind them displaying the 'BEST MEN'S CLUB' graphic and 'SEVILLA' prominently, as the audience, some capturing the event on their phones, applauds. The formal and celebratory atmosphere, enhanced by golden podiums and dynamic lighting, concludes with a man in a black suit walking confidently down the stage amidst continued applause, likely the award recipient.

The Nakheel Presents Dubai Globe Soccer Awards 2023 unfolds with a vibrant stage backdrop of pulsing orange and yellow geometric light patterns, set to the continuous sound of crowd applause. Tom Urquhart, in a black tuxedo with a bow tie, stands at the golden podium, speaking into a lapel microphone and gesturing, his clear male voice announcing, "The club, who also lifted the FA Cup for the seventh time in their history." The camera then cuts to a man in a black suit holding a large, ornate silver trophy with blue and white ribbons, walking confidently across the stage, soon joined by another man in a black suit with a similar trophy, standing side by side. Amanda Davies, elegantly dressed in a strapless black gown with a diamond necklace, appears at the golden podium, holding a blue folder with the event's logo, engaging with Tom. Her clear female voice declares, "The undefeated winners of the UEFA Champions League!" as the stage backdrop dynamically displays 'BEST MEN'S CLUB' alongside Dubai Sports Council and Nakheel logos. Tom Urquhart continues, "Which of course allowed them to go on to win the UEFA Super Cup for the very first time!" The two men with trophies are then joined by a third, forming a line of three, their synchronized movements and the gleaming trophies highlighted under the stage lights. Amanda Davies concludes, her voice announcing, "And the team who landed the FIFA Club World Cup on their tournament debut!" as a man in a black suit walks across the stage with a trophy, emphasizing the celebratory atmosphere of the awards ceremony.

The segment opens at the Nakheel Dubai Globe Soccer Awards with three men in black suits, holding ornate silver trophies adorned with blue and white ribbons, walking confidently onto a stage beside a host in a black tuxedo at a golden podium. The stage backdrop dynamically displays 'BEST MEN'S CLUB' with glowing orange and yellow geometric patterns. The host, speaking into a microphone, builds suspense, asking "So, any ideas?" before dramatically announcing, "Well, the Best Men's Club are none other than Manchester City!" This declaration is met with enthusiastic applause and cheering from the formally dressed audience, who are seen capturing the moment on their phones. The scene then transitions to a dynamic football highlight montage, accompanied by an upbeat, celebratory soundtrack and a narrator's voice. The montage begins with a player in a red and white striped jersey scoring, overlaid with text proclaiming 'FIRST ENGLISH SIDE TO WIN 5 MAJOR TROPHIES IN A CALENDAR YEAR'. Further footage shows a Manchester City player in a light blue jersey scoring, with text highlighting their 'WON 28 OF 38 PREMIER LEAGUE GAMES WITH A 73% WIN RATIO'. The narrator details their "global success," stating, "Victory over Inter Milan, City landed the UEFA Champions League title for the very first time," calling it "a culmination of years of hard work." The montage continues with the team celebrating on a confetti-strewn podium, then lifting the UEFA Champions League trophy, as text confirms they were 'UNBEATEN IN WINNING THEIR FIRST CHAMPIONS LEAGUE & FIFA CLUB WORLD CUP TITLES'. The narrator concludes by explaining that "Victory over Fluminense, the final piece of the puzzle was placed. As City won the FIFA Club World Cup," making them "the first English club to simultaneously hold the Premier League, FA Cup, UEFA Champions League, UEFA Super Cup, and the FIFA..." before the audio fades.

event_blocks.summary: The Dubai Globe Soccer Awards ceremony commenced with a montage celebrating Manchester City's 'sporting immortality' following their UEFA Champions League and Club World Cup victories, transitioning to an aerial view of Atlantis The Palm Hotel with a 'BEST MEN'S CLUB MANCHESTER CITY' graphic. Inside the venue, host Amanda Davies presented the 'Best Men's Club' award to Manchester City, with players and officials accepting a large, golden, globe-shaped trophy on a stage adorned with the Nakheel Dubai Globe Soccer Awards logo and Manchester City's crest. Rodrigo, holding a trophy, was interviewed by Davies, discussing the significance of the Champions League win, with Erling Haaland also present. Rodrigo reflected on his nine years with the team and the 'heartache' of previous Champions League campaigns, expressing gratitude to his teammates. The Premier League trophy was also showcased. Tom Urquhart and Amanda Davies then introduced the 'BEST MIDDLE EAST PLAYER' award, sponsored by Italpreziosi, with finalists Cristiano Ronaldo, Riyad Mahrez, and Mohamed Elshenawy announced. Cristiano Ronaldo was revealed as the winner, receiving the award and the Maradona Award, noted for its replica of Diego Maradona's foot, for his 'magical year' and 'world-leading 54 goals' for Al Nassr and Portugal. Ronaldo, in a navy blue suit, expressed pride in the award, calling it a 'good moment' and his 'first one' in the Middle East, thanking his teammates, coaches, wife, and kids, and stating his intention to display the trophy in his museum. He also discussed the growth of football in Saudi Arabia, expressing confidence in the league's future development.

raw_segments: The segment opens with a vibrant montage celebrating Manchester City's recent triumphs, showing players on a podium lifting the UEFA Champions League trophy amidst a shower of confetti and fireworks in a packed stadium, as a male voiceover declares their 'sporting immortality' after securing the Club World Cup. This transitions to a stunning nighttime aerial view of the Atlantis The Palm Hotel, illuminated in blue and pink, with a dynamic graphic overlay announcing 'BEST MEN'S CLUB MANCHESTER CITY' over the iconic structure, accompanied by upbeat, celebratory background music. The scene then shifts to the grand interior of the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards venue, where a female host, identified as Amanda Davies, enthusiastically announces, 'What a season, what a trophy haul! Manchester City deservedly named Best Men's Club.' Formally dressed men, including players and officials in tuxedos, are seen walking through the applauding audience towards a golden-lit stage. Amanda Davies continues, inviting 'the rest of the team to come up and join us on stage,' specifically calling out 'Kevin' to 'come on over' and 'get your hands on another trophy.' On stage, a Manchester City player in a black suit, presumably Kevin, receives a large, golden, globe-shaped trophy from a dignitary in traditional Emirati attire, while Amanda Davies presents the award. The on-screen text confirms 'Manchester City Best Men's Club of the Year,' with the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo and Manchester City's crest prominently displayed on the stage backdrop, as the audience continues to applaud and capture the moment on their phones.

The segment opens with a wide shot of the grand stage at the Nakheel Presents Dubai Globe Soccer Awards, where formally dressed individuals, including footballers and dignitaries, stand holding trophies under golden light patterns highlighting the 'BEST MEN'S CLUB' award and Manchester City's crest. A female voice, likely the host, encourages, "Come on, come on in, round for the team pick, everybody," followed by "Huddle in, huddle in." The audience, dressed in formal attire and traditional keffiyehs, applauds loudly as a male announcer exclaims, "So let's hear it one more time, give them a round of applause, your team at the end, Manchester City!" This is accompanied by celebratory music and continued applause. The camera then shows close-ups of clapping audience members before returning to the stage, where Amanda Davies, in a black strapless gown and diamond necklace, is seen holding a microphone and interviewing Rodrigo, who is in a black suit and holds a golden globe-shaped trophy. Behind him, Erling Haaland is visible in a striking blue tuxedo. Amanda Davies is heard asking, "So Kevin, we touched on it a little bit earlier, but this was the big one, wasn't it, for Manchester City, getting your hands on on the Champions League at last. What does that mean to you personally?" Rodrigo, seen speaking into the microphone, responds in a calm, reflective voice, explaining, "Um, well, it's the one that I missed with this team, you know. Um, I've been here for nine years and it's been a lot of uh, heartache, um, going through the Champions League, but you know, last year was uh, amazing and yeah, these guys helped me win this trophy, so I'm very grateful to everybody from the club for for the guys and..." During his speech, the camera zooms in on the UEFA Champions League trophy and later the Premier League trophy, both held by recipients in black suits, showcasing their silver surfaces and blue ribbons, all bathed in the warm, golden, celebratory lighting of the event.

The segment opens with a close-up of Amanda Davies, elegant in a strapless black gown with a diamond necklace, holding a microphone as she interviews Rodrigo, who is dressed in a black double-breasted suit and holds a golden globe-shaped trophy. Rodrigo's composed voice is heard reflecting on his team's performance, stating, "at least I got one," and later expressing confidence, "these guys are amazing... this team is ready to go again." Behind them, Erling Haaland, striking in a blue tuxedo, smiles. Amanda, addressing Rodrigo as "Gonn," playfully asks about his hair, to which he humorously replies, "Just don't go to the barber," eliciting laughter from Amanda and others. A brief cutaway shows a large, ornate silver Premier League trophy, adorned with blue and white ribbons, held by a man in a black suit. Returning to the interview, Amanda's clear voice congratulates "Manchester City and Kevin De Bruyne," as the audience, including men in traditional white keffiyehs, applauds enthusiastically. The camera then pulls back to reveal the full, golden-lit 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' stage, with glowing geometric designs and multiple formally dressed attendees holding trophies, all contributing to the glamorous and celebratory atmosphere, with an unidentified male voice also heard congratulating "the entire team" amidst the sustained applause and swelling background music.

Against a backdrop of pulsing orange and yellow geometric light patterns, Tom Urquhart, a man in a black tuxedo with a bow tie, stands at a golden circular podium displaying the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo. Speaking into a lapel microphone with a clear male British accent, he thanks Manchester City before transitioning to the next award, highlighting the presence of "world champions and continental champions" and "global superstars right here in the Middle East." The scene then cuts to Amanda Davies, a woman in a strapless black gown with subtle sparkles and a diamond necklace, also at the golden podium with a bottle of water beside her. With a clear female British accent, she adds that a "plethora of homegrown talent" makes "football in the Middle East... at its most formidable," evidenced by "brilliant performances over the last year." The camera returns to Tom Urquhart, who, with arms crossed then gesturing, asks, "who has been the player who delivered the very best and most consistently?" He then announces the award is "kindly sponsored by our friends at Italpreziosi" and prepares to reveal the finalists, all set to a consistent, upbeat orchestral background music. A graphic then transitions onto the screen, announcing the 'BEST MIDDLE EAST PLAYER' award with the 'ITALPREZIOSI' logo. Tom Urquhart's voice declares, "And the finalists then are..." as the nominees are displayed one by one in vertical frames against a glowing backdrop: Cristiano Ronaldo in a yellow Al Nassr jersey, Riyad Mahrez in a dark green Al-Ahli jersey, and Mohamed Elshenawy in a red Al Ahly jersey, each accompanied by applause as their names are spoken.

The video opens with a golden animated graphic announcing the 'BEST MIDDLE EAST PLAYER' award, flanked by 'ITALPREZIOSI' logos, as a male voice announces "Riyad Mahrez" and then "Mohamed Al Shenawy," while Mohamed Elshenawy is shown in a red Al Ahly jersey. The scene transitions to a wide shot of the formally dressed audience, seated in white armchairs, some capturing the event on their phones. Amanda Davies, in a sparkling black gown and diamond necklace, stands at the golden circular podium with the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo behind her, speaking into a microphone. She addresses the audience, announcing, "Ladies and gentlemen, the winner of the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards Best Middle East Player..." and builds suspense, stating, "I think the roof is about to go off. It's none other than... Cristiano Ronaldo!" This dramatic reveal is met with immediate, enthusiastic audience applause and cheers, accompanied by swelling music. Cristiano Ronaldo is seen in the audience, in a navy blue suit, attentively watching the stage, while the woman beside him smiles. The camera cuts back to Amanda Davies, with a large screen behind her displaying 'CRISTIANO RONALDO,' as she continues to praise his "magical year," mentioning his "54 goals in 2023" and that he "also secures you the Maradona Award, brought to you by Nakheel." She then highlights an "extraordinary" detail: "this trophy actually has the exact replica of the late great Diego Maradona's foot," which elicits further audience applause, as the scene alternates between her at the podium and the engaged audience.

The segment opens with a dynamic montage celebrating Cristiano Ronaldo's prolific scoring record, as a male narrator details his career, stating he is "further adding to his legacy by taking his talent to Al Nassr in Saudi Arabia" and that his "foresight made the globalization of football a reality." Text overlays like 'SCORED 54 GOALS IN 59 GAMES' and '44 GOALS FOR AL NASSR, 10 GOALS FOR PORTUGAL' flash over fast-paced clips of Ronaldo scoring and celebrating goals in his yellow and blue Al Nassr jersey, with close-ups and wide shots enhanced by sparks and fiery effects. The narrator emphasizes Ronaldo's "prowess on the pitch" and his "ability to make revolutionary decisions off it," concluding that "2023 ended with CR7 netting a staggering, world-leading 54 goals" for both Al Nassr and the Portuguese national team. The visuals then transition to a nighttime aerial view of the Atlantis The Palm Hotel, illuminated with blue and pink lights, establishing the Dubai setting. The scene shifts to the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards ceremony, where Ronaldo, dressed in a navy blue suit and black turtleneck, walks confidently down the stage aisle toward the podium. The grand stage features a dark backdrop with glowing orange and yellow geometric patterns, the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo, and large screens displaying Ronaldo's image. He receives the 'Best Middle East Player of the Year and Maradona Award' from a man in a green thobe and white keffiyeh, shaking hands and posing for photos with the golden globe-shaped trophy. The audience, seated in white armchairs, applauds as Ronaldo walks back to his seat, while a male voice exclaims, "Another Globe Soccer Award for Cristiano!" and a female voice adds, "Congratulations, Cristiano!"

The video segment opens with Amanda Davies, dressed in a strapless black gown with subtle sparkles and a diamond necklace, holding a microphone and a blue folder, engaging Cristiano Ronaldo on stage. Her clear voice is heard asking Ronaldo about the importance of his first goal in Saudi Arabia, referencing past discussions about using criticism as motivation. Ronaldo, in a navy blue suit over a black turtleneck, stands beside a golden globe-shaped trophy on a black pedestal, with dynamic orange and yellow geometric light patterns animating the stage backdrop. As the camera zooms in, Ronaldo takes the microphone, his composed and reflective expression occasionally breaking into a warm smile. His voice, with a slight accent, confirms he remembers the goal, calling it a "good moment" and his "first one," expressing immense pride in his "amazing season." With a diamond earring glinting and neatly styled hair, he extends gratitude to his teammates and coaches from both Al Nassr and the Portugal National Team. Ronaldo continues, expressing pride in the golden trophy, which he identifies as the "best Middle East player" award, noting he'd never won it before and plans to display it passionately in his museum. He concludes by thanking his wife and kids for dealing with him, all while the vibrant stage lights continue to pulse in the background, maintaining the glamorous and formal ambiance.

The scene opens with a wide shot of the formally dressed audience at the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards, seated in white armchairs, with some attendees, including a man in a traditional white keffiyeh and a woman in a white lace dress, capturing the event on their phones. The visual then transitions to the stage where Amanda Davies, in a strapless black gown and diamond necklace, holds a microphone and a blue folder, engaging in conversation with Cristiano Ronaldo, who is dressed in a navy blue suit over a black turtleneck. Ronaldo's composed and reflective voice is heard, stating, "Sometimes I know I'm a little bit boring, but it's it's my job, you know, to be consistent every time. So, thank you to the Global Soccer for this amazing award." The stage backdrop features glowing orange and yellow geometric light patterns, with the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo faintly visible. Amanda Davies then asks, "And you have said, you know, this is about more than just you. This is about growing football in the game in another region. How confident are you that the influx of players will continue?" The camera zooms in on Ronaldo, his diamond earring visible, as he responds, "I hope so. As I say before, the league... start one year ago to grow and grow. And I'm sure 100% they will continue to grow because our our boss, they decide and they love football. And in my opinion, the league will be top top top." The scene widens to show the full stage with the 'esaaD' logo illuminated behind them. Amanda Davies then exclaims, "Wow, top top top. You've got another trophy to collect if I can just usher you back over there. This is the pretty special Maradona award complete with a replica of the foot." A man in a green thobe and white keffiyeh then presents Ronaldo with a golden trophy, which Ronaldo accepts with a smile as the stage lights pulse rhythmically, creating a celebratory atmosphere.

event_blocks.summary: The Dubai Globe Soccer Awards ceremony commenced with an audience, including individuals in traditional attire, applauding as Amanda Davies congratulated an unseen recipient. The stage, adorned with 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' branding and dynamic geometric lighting, hosted the Host in Black Tuxedo who acknowledged Cristiano Ronaldo. The 'Best Coach' award nominees – Marcel Koller, Josep Guardiola, and Carlo Ancelotti – were then presented through animated graphics and vertical frames featuring their club crests. Following this, Tom Urquhart announced Pep Guardiola as the winner, triggering widespread cheers. A montage showcased Guardiola's achievements with Manchester City, highlighting his tactical prowess and historic wins like the Premier League hat-trick and FIFA Club World Cup finals, with on-screen text detailing his 'FIRST MANAGER TO WIN 4 FIFA CLUB WORLD CUP FINALS' and 'SECOND COACH TO WIN A HAT-TRICK OF PREMIER LEAGUE TITLES'. The narrative emphasized his evolution from Barcelona and Munich to Manchester City. The celebration continued with footage of Manchester City lifting the Premier League and UEFA Champions League trophies, underscoring their treble and Guardiola being the 'FIRST MANAGER TO WIN A TREBLE TWICE'. Guardiola, holding the UEFA Champions League trophy against the backdrop of Atlantis The Palm Hotel, was then shown walking onto the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards stage to accept the 'Best Coach of the Year' award, a golden globe, from a Recipient in Green Thobe. Amanda Davies congratulated him, and as they exited, Davies placed a hand on his shoulder while he held the trophy. Later, Davies interviewed Guardiola, who, holding the trophy, reflected on winning, attributing success to his team and the UAE's support, and discussed the difficulty of comparing his Barcelona and Manchester City teams, likening it to choosing between parents. The audience, including Erling Haaland, observed the proceedings.

Her clear, articulate voice is heard throughout the segment, hosting the event and interviewing Guardiola.

raw_segments: The segment opens with a wide shot of the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards audience, including a man in a traditional white keffiyeh and a woman in a white lace dress, all seated in white armchairs and clapping enthusiastically. A female voice, identified as Amanda Davies, congratulates someone, followed by the sound of sustained audience applause and celebratory music. The scene transitions to a stage illuminated with dynamic orange and yellow geometric light patterns, featuring the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo and sponsor logos. A male voice, belonging to the Host in Black Tuxedo, then congratulates Cristiano Ronaldo, noting he'll need "all his hands there for the two trophies" as he exits the stage. The Host continues, describing the qualities of a "tactical genius" and announcing that the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards will present an "esteemed list of best coach nominees." Amanda Davies then takes over, her voice declaring, "Your finalists for the Best Coach of the Year are..." as dramatic music builds. The video cuts to a graphic sequence for the 'BEST COACH' award, displaying nominees Marcel Koller, Josep Guardiola, and Carlo Ancelotti in vertical frames with their respective club crests against a dark background with shimmering golden light effects and floating orbs. Amanda Davies' voice announces "Marcel Koller" as the first nominee, accompanied by renewed audience applause and continuing music.

The segment opens with a dynamic animated sequence for the 'BEST COACH' award, featuring glowing orange and yellow geometric light patterns and floating orbs against a dark background, accompanied by upbeat, celebratory music. As the music plays, nominees are introduced in vertical frames: Carlo Ancelotti, in a grey suit with the Real Madrid crest, is announced by name, followed by Mikel Arteta in a black jacket with the Arsenal crest, also announced by name. Lionel Scaloni, in a blue Argentina national team polo with the Argentina crest, is then named, and finally, Simone Inzaghi, in a white shirt and black tie with the Inter Milan crest, is introduced. The scene transitions to a formally dressed audience seated in white armchairs, some clapping and others observing attentively. Tom Urquhart, a man in a black tuxedo with a bow tie, stands at a golden circular podium bearing the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo, speaking into a lapel microphone. He announces, with a clear, formal tone, "Okay then, the winner... and the Best Coach of the Globe Soccer Awards is..." The camera returns to the audience, showing close-ups of individuals clapping and reacting enthusiastically as Urquhart declares, "Pep Guardiola!" This is met with widespread applause and cheering. The video then cuts to a montage of Josép Guardiola, initially in a white shirt with the Manchester City crest, as a narrator's voice begins, describing his impact: "With fundamental surrounding possession, pressing, creativity, Pep Guardiola has been at the forefront of the beautiful game's evolution." The montage continues with celebratory footage of Guardiola lifting trophies and being hoisted by his team, featuring dynamic camera angles and fiery visual effects, while on-screen text highlights his achievements: 'FIRST MANAGER TO WIN 4 FIFA CLUB WORLD CUP FINALS' and 'SECOND COACH TO WIN A HAT-TRICK OF PREMIER LEAGUE TITLES'. The narrator concludes, "Having stamped his authority in Barcelona and Munich, Guardiola's status was further elevated in Manchester City."

The video opens with a vibrant celebration of Manchester City's Premier League triumph, showing players in light blue jerseys joyfully lifting the Premier League trophy amidst a shower of confetti under a stadium arch, set to triumphant, energetic music and crowd cheering. The scene transitions to a sepia-toned montage of the team celebrating the UEFA Champions League victory, with the trophy held high and confetti falling, emphasizing their historic treble. A narrator's voice states, "After dominating the Premier League, he secured European then global success in 2023. The Champions League in June, followed by the Club World Cup triumph." On-screen text highlights Pep Guardiola as the 'FIRST MANAGER TO WIN A TREBLE TWICE' with Barcelona (2008–2009) and Manchester City (2022–2023). The narrator continues, "This is a coach who has an obsessive eye for detail, with the ability to get the very best out of every player he's worked with. 2023 was a special year for a special coach." The narrative then shifts to a close-up of Guardiola’s emotional face, followed by a dramatic shot of him holding the UEFA Champions League trophy against the illuminated backdrop of Atlantis The Palm Hotel in Dubai, with the text 'BEST COACH PEP GUARDIOLA' displayed, all accompanied by the continuing triumphant music. The scene transitions to the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards ceremony, where Guardiola, dressed in a black suit, walks onto the stage past an audibly applauding audience. He receives the 'Best Coach of the Year' award, a golden globe-shaped trophy, from a man in a green thobe and white keffiyeh, shaking hands and posing for photos. The stage is adorned with glowing orange and yellow geometric patterns, and the backdrop features the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo. A woman, Amanda Davies, in a black strapless gown, congratulates him, saying, "Congratulations. Let me just bring you back this way a little bit," as applause swells. Guardiola then walks off stage with Amanda, who places a hand on his shoulder as they exit, the golden trophy in his hand reflecting the stage lights, with Amanda adding, "Thank you."

The segment opens with a close-up of Amanda Davies, elegantly dressed in a sparkling black gown and diamond necklace, standing beside Pep Guardiola, who is sharp in a black suit and white shirt, holding a large, golden, globe-shaped trophy. Davies, holding a black microphone, places a hand on Guardiola’s shoulder as they walk off stage, smiling amidst the dynamic, pulsing orange and yellow geometric light patterns of the stage backdrop, which displays sponsor logos like 'ITALPREZIOSI' and 'ATLANTIS THE PALM DUBAI'. Davies's clear, articulate voice is heard congratulating Guardiola, "Firstly, happy birthday. Not a bad birthday celebration, getting your hands on another trophy. But what does it feel like being the best in the world?" Guardiola, speaking with a calm, reflective tone, responds, "Because we won. If we don't win, we are not even invited, so that is the truth. To win, there are many, many reasons, especially all of them are sitting there. And of course, we are here in UAE, where I would have been helped unconditionally. Yeah, really, really happy." The camera briefly cuts to the formally dressed audience, including Erling Haaland in a striking blue tuxedo, observing with a relaxed posture. Returning to the stage, Davies continues her interview, asking, "How much do you allow yourself to celebrate the successes?" Guardiola, gesturing with his free hand while holding the trophy, replies, "A lot. But you know, it's on to next as quick as possible, but of course we celebrate it. And especially when the days come through, the years come through, you'll remember. They will be not forgotten for all of us, that's for sure." The stage backdrop now shows 'SERIE A MADE IN ITALY' and 'MBA' logos. Davies then asks, "And have you managed to come up with an answer yet, which is the best, your Barcelona team or this one?" Guardiola chuckles slightly, concluding, "It's like us mom and dad to choose." The atmosphere remains warm and celebratory, acknowledging Guardiola's 'Best Coach of the Year' award.

event_blocks.summary: Amanda Davies, adorned in a strapless black gown and diamond necklace, interviews Pep Guardiola on a stage bathed in dynamic orange and yellow geometric light patterns and sponsor logos, with Guardiola holding a golden globe-shaped trophy. After Davies playfully remarks on being a 'daddy's girl' and congratulates Guardiola, urging him to 'Go and join the team,' celebratory music and applause commence. The camera shifts to the formally attired audience, including individuals in traditional white keffiyehs and women in elegant gowns, seated in white armchairs and applauding. A wide shot emphasizes the venue's grandeur. Tom Urquhart, sporting a black tuxedo and bow tie, approaches a golden circular podium bearing the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBE SOCCER AWARDS' logo, speaking into a lapel microphone. He congratulates Pep and the Man City team before announcing the 'Career Award,' sponsored by Audi Al Nabooda Automobiles, describing the recipient as embodying 'inspiration, drive, and that all-important never-say-die attitude.' Urquhart then exclaims 'Casemiro!' eliciting loud applause and cheering from the audience, notably from Kyle Walker and a woman in a sparkling white gown. The segment culminates with a dynamic graphic showcasing a footballer's face, overlaid with the bold orange and white text '3 X FIFA CLUB WORLD CUP WINNER,' enhanced by fiery visual effects and a glowing soccer ball animation.

event_blocks.summary: The Dubai Globe Soccer Awards ceremony commenced with Casemiro, speaking from a golden podium on a dynamically lit stage, expressing gratitude in Portuguese and dedicating his award to his wife, Ana, before Amanda Davies translated his sentiments, noting his ambition for more wins. The event then shifted focus to the 'Best President' award, with Tom Urquhart and Amanda Davies introducing nominees Khaldoon Al Mubarak, Joan Laporta, and José Castro. Khaldoon Al Mubarak of Manchester City was announced as the winner by Amanda Davies, receiving a golden trophy from a Presenter in Dark Suit, with on-screen text confirming his award. The Presenter and Amanda Davies lauded Khaldoon's leadership, highlighting Manchester City's success, including five trophies and record revenue, and the club's 15-year journey to the summit of world football. Khaldoon Al Mubarak, in a white thobe, expressed his gratitude, attributing the club's success to the best players, coaching staff, organization, football director Chiki Begiristan, and CEO Ferran Soriano, and acknowledging His Highness Sheikh Mansour bin Mohammed bin Rashid. He further elaborated on Manchester City's 'hunger' and 'urge' for excellence, citing their DNA, culture, seven Premier Leagues in 11 years, three in a row, and being the first team since 1999 to win a treble, with aspirations for a fourth consecutive Premier League and another treble. The ceremony continued with Tom Urquhart and Amanda Davies presenting the 'Fan's Favourite Player of the Year' award, with nominees Cristiano Ronaldo, Lionel Messi, and Mohamed Salah. Cristiano Ronaldo was announced as the winner by Amanda Davies, with 5 million votes cast, leading to a montage celebrating his career, highlighting his record 873 goals, international scoring and appearance records, and his significant impact on Middle Eastern football.

event_blocks.summary: The Dubai Globe Soccer Awards commenced with a dynamic montage celebrating Cristiano Ronaldo's career achievements, featuring on-screen text highlighting his goal-scoring records and international appearances, set against upbeat, celebratory music. The event then shifted to a nighttime aerial view of Atlantis The Palm Hotel, establishing the Dubai setting, before cutting to the Nakheel Dubai Globe Soccer Awards venue. Ronaldo, in a navy blue suit and black turtleneck, accepted the 'Fan’s Favourite Player of the Year' award from a man in a green thobe, posing for photos on a stage adorned with glowing geometric light patterns and sponsor logos. He delivered an acceptance speech from a golden podium, emphasizing the fans' passion and importance, stating, "is the people who go to the stadiums to see goals, to see good good games." The ceremony continued with Tom Urquhart, in a black tuxedo, introducing the 'BEST MEN'S PLAYER' award, highlighting the past year's football achievements. Amanda Davies, in a sparkling black gown, announced the nominees: Cristiano Ronaldo, Erling Haaland, and Mohamed Salah. The award for 'Best Men's Player of the Year' was ultimately presented to Erling Haaland, who walked confidently to the stage in a royal blue suit and white bow tie to receive a golden, globe-shaped trophy from two men in traditional Emirati thobes. Haaland, after accepting the award, was interviewed by Amanda Davies, discussing his seamless integration into Manchester City, praising the club's people, manager, and administration, and confirming his Champions League ringtone. The interview concluded with Haaland expressing his eagerness to return to playing soon. The ceremony also featured a split-screen showing Cristiano Ronaldo and Erling Haaland observing the proceedings, and a brief glimpse of Marcel Koller in the audience.
