Ronaldo, with a composed and reflective demeanor, speaks into the microphones, stating with a slight accent, "She asked very difficult questions. So, as you know, for me the most important thing in football, it's to make the fans happy because is the people who go to the stadiums to see goals, to see good good games. And if they vote, is because they have passion for football. For me it's a special award because the most important..." as the audio fades. The audience, including men in traditional white keffiyehs and women in elegant gowns, watches attentively, while the backdrop behind him features a large screen with 'KAFD' and 'CRISTIANO RONALDO' in bold white letters, with dynamic orange light streaks pulsing across the stage.

Cristiano Ronaldo, sharply dressed in a navy blue suit and black turtleneck, stands at a golden circular podium, delivering an acceptance speech into two microphones. He speaks passionately, with a slight accent, about the importance of fans, recalling the challenges of playing in empty stadiums during COVID, stating, "without you guys, the football is nothing." He expresses gratitude for being voted for, prompting loud applause and cheers from the formally dressed audience, many of whom are recording on their phones. Ronaldo then encourages continued support for football, Al Nassr, and himself, eliciting further cheers. As upbeat, celebratory music begins to play, a female voice announces, "Congratulations, Cristiano Ronaldo, ladies and gentlemen." The camera pulls back to reveal the grand stage setup, featuring multiple podiums, sponsor logos like 'NAKHEEL' and 'AUDI', and a central screen displaying 'DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS'. Two other figures, one in a green thobe and another in a white thobe, stand beside him. The scene then shifts to Tom Urquhart, in a black tuxedo, speaking into a lapel microphone at a similar golden podium, his clear, formal British accent introducing, "And so, my friends, it comes down to this, the grand finale, the final..." against the dynamic orange and yellow light patterns of the stage.

The segment opens with Tom Urquhart, dressed in a black tuxedo with a bow tie, standing at a golden circular podium bearing the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo. He speaks into a lapel microphone, gesturing with his hands, thanking the "millions that have tuned in around the world" and describing the past year in football as "from the sublime to the magical" and "downright astonishing," highlighting "defensive resilience, midfield mastery, goal scoring supremacy," all against a backdrop of pulsing orange and yellow geometric light patterns and accompanied by triumphant orchestral music. After a few moments, Amanda Davies, wearing a sparkling strapless black gown and diamond necklace, appears at the same podium, smiling warmly. She announces, "So, it is time to crown the best of the best. Your nominees are..." as the music swells. The video then cuts to a golden animated graphic for the 'BEST MEN'S PLAYER' award, featuring shimmering light trails and floating orbs. Amanda Davies then announces each nominee as they appear in vertical frames against the dynamic golden background: "Cristiano Ronaldo!" in a yellow Al Nassr jersey, followed by "Erling Haaland!" in a blue Manchester City jersey, and finally "Mohamed Salah!" in a red Liverpool jersey, each announcement met with crowd applause and cheers.

The segment opens with an energetic, dramatic musical score accompanying a dynamic animated sequence for the 'BEST MEN'S PLAYER' award. Vertical frames against a dark, shimmering golden background showcase nominees, with a female voice distinctly announcing each name: first, Jude Bellingham in his Real Madrid jersey, followed by Bernardo Silva in a Manchester City jersey, then Lionel Messi in an Inter Miami jersey, Karim Benzema in an Al Ittihad jersey, Kylian Mbappé in a Paris Saint-Germain jersey, Victor Osimhen in a Napoli jersey, and finally, Vinícius Jr. in a Real Madrid jersey. Each player's name and club crest are visible beneath their image. The scene then transitions to a wide shot of the formally dressed audience, including some in traditional Emirati attire, seated in white armchairs. The camera cuts to a split-screen, showing Cristiano Ronaldo in a dark blue suit and Erling Haaland in a bright blue suit, both seated with serious, observant expressions. Tom Urquhart, the host, is then seen at a golden circular podium with the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo, speaking into a lapel microphone against a backdrop of pulsing orange and yellow geometric light patterns. His voice is heard, remarking, "It is quite the list, is it not? But one of those names has been crowned the final winner of the evening." He continues, addressing the "ladies, gentlemen, honored guests," as the split-screen of Ronaldo and Haaland returns, capturing their reactions. Urquhart then announces, "Best Men's Player of the Year at this year's Nakheel Dubai Globe Soccer Award goes to..." building anticipation as the music swells and audience applause begins.

The video opens with a split-screen view of the audience at the Nakheel Dubai Globe Soccer Awards, showing Cristiano Ronaldo in a dark blue suit, resting his chin on his hand, and Erling Haaland in a bright blue suit with a white bow tie, sitting upright. Upbeat, celebratory music plays as a male voice shouts "Haaland!" then "Erling Haaland!", followed by applause, coinciding with a close-up of Haaland clapping. The scene transitions to a dynamic montage of Haaland's football highlights as a narrator begins, "With a combination of power, pace, and intricate movement," explaining that "Erling Haaland has scored against some of the world's best defenses." The narrator continues, stating that "Under the tutelage of Pep Guardiola, Haaland scored a Premier League record 36 goals in 35 league appearances in his debut season," while on-screen text confirms him as the 'TOP SCORER IN THE PREMIER LEAGUE WITH 36 GOALS IN 35 APPEARANCES,' accompanied by footage of him celebrating and scoring goals. The montage continues with on-screen text announcing him as 'TOP SCORER IN THE CHAMPIONS LEAGUE WITH 12 GOALS IN 11 APPEARANCES,' showcasing powerful strikes and goal celebrations, as the narrator adds that he "also finishes as the top scorer in City's triumphant run to the Champions League glory with 12 goals," describing him as a "once-in-a-generation goalscorer" seamlessly fitting into a team of creative talents. A rapid sequence of goals is shown, culminating in the text '50 GOALS IN 2023,' emphasizing his prolific scoring, with the narrator concluding that these "50 goals in 2023 helped City win five trophies in the year." The video ends with Haaland raising his fist in triumph against a backdrop of Dubai's skyline, including Atlantis The Palm Hotel, accompanied by a "whoosh" sound effect before transitioning to a stage shot. On stage, with the music swelling and applause, a male announcer declares, "Men's Player of the Year, Erling Haaland!", as the award graphic 'MEN'S PLAYER OF THE YEAR' with his name in bold gold letters is displayed.

The video opens with Erling Haaland, striking in a royal blue double-breasted suit with a white bow tie and his blonde hair slicked back in a bun, walking confidently down the aisle towards the stage at the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards. Upbeat, celebratory music plays as a male announcer's voice declares, "So the final award of the evening, the crowning glory, the best men's player of the year. This year's Dubai Globe Soccer Awards brought to you by Nakheel. Erling Haaland!" The grand hall is filled with formally dressed audience members, many in traditional Emirati attire, who erupt in loud applause and cheers as Haaland ascends the stage. The stage backdrop glows with dynamic orange and yellow geometric light patterns and displays event branding like 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' and sponsor logos including Audi and SUSHISAMBA DUBAI. He is greeted by two men in traditional Emirati thobes—one in green and one in white—who present him with a large, golden, globe-shaped trophy. On-screen text identifies the award: 'Erling Haaland Best Men's Player of the Year'. After a brief acknowledgment, Haaland steps aside as the camera pans to the still-applauding audience. He then joins Amanda Davies, the host in a strapless black gown with a diamond necklace, for an on-stage interview. She holds a microphone and a blue folder, asking Haaland, "Erling, congratulations. Just a couple of words if I may. It's almost easy to forget at times that it was only last season you joined City. What is it about this club, squad, this team that has made it so easy for you to settle in and shine?" Haaland, speaking into the mic with a distinct, slightly deep voice and Norwegian accent, gestures subtly with his hands as he replies, "Well, I think first, first of all, the people... the guys that sits here is, uh, is a bunch of amazing people. Uh, and then you have the, the manager, of course, who is, uh, is not so bad as well. Uh, so, uh, yeah, it's a, it's a fantastic club and, um," The stage lighting remains vibrant with pulsing orange and yellow effects, and sponsor logos are visible in the background, maintaining the celebratory and formal atmosphere of his recognition.

On stage at the Nakheel Presents Dubai Globe Soccer Awards, Amanda Davies, elegant in a strapless black gown with subtle sparkles and a diamond necklace, holds a microphone and gestures as she interviews Erling Haaland. Haaland, in a bright blue double-breasted suit with a white bow tie and his blonde hair in a bun, stands beside her, hands clasped, occasionally smiling and nodding. The stage backdrop features dynamic, pulsing orange and yellow geometric light patterns, with sponsor logos like 'KOORA BREAK' and 'Audi' visible. Haaland's distinct, accented voice is heard explaining, "...the support we players get from from the administration and and everything and the leaders in the club is is massive as well. So, uh, it's a nice club and uh and I really enjoy being here." Amanda, speaking in a clear, engaging voice, then asks, "Not a bad job at all. Have you still got the Champions League as your ringtone on your phone now you've won it and done it?" Haaland confirms, "Yeah, I do. Uh, and uh I uh I love this competition. Uh, and yeah, it was a was a big dream when when we won it. Uh, and uh fantastic feeling." Amanda follows up, "Do you know when we're going to see you back?" to which Haaland replies, "I hope really soon because I'm getting a bit sick of uh not playing. So, uh, hopefully soon." Amanda concludes, "Great. We wish you well with your recovery and congratulations once again." She then addresses the audience, "Ladies and gentlemen, Erling, oh! Oh, hang on a minute, I got a bit carried away. Before we let you go, we've got some special messages." During this exchange, the camera briefly cuts to a close-up of Marcel Koller in the audience, observing with a serious expression, while the focused lighting maintains the glamorous and celebratory atmosphere.

event_blocks.summary: Amanda Davies, adorned in a sparkling black gown and diamond necklace, stood on stage beside Erling Haaland, who wore a bright blue double-breasted suit with his blonde hair in a bun, against a backdrop of pulsing orange and yellow geometric lights. As Davies stated, 'from some of your fans, just have a look,' upbeat, celebratory music commenced, underscoring a montage. This montage featured young boys in blue and black striped 'DHL' football kits striking Haaland’s iconic meditation pose on sunlit pitches, interspersed with match footage of Haaland scoring goals, accompanied by commentary like 'Erling Haaland finish!' and on-screen text. A chorus of children's voices in multiple languages enthusiastically shouted variations of 'Congrats Haaland!', 'Congratulations Haaland!', 'Mabrook Haaland!', 'Bravo Haaland!', and 'Congratulazioni Haaland!'. The scene then shifted to a dusty field in Malawi, where a young boy in an orange bib and others replicated the meditation pose, demonstrating Haaland's global influence. Returning to Dubai, Haaland smiled as he departed the stage with Davies amidst the celebratory audio. The camera then focused on the audience, where Pep Guardiola, in a black suit, clapped thoughtfully, alongside other formally dressed attendees, including men in white keffiyehs, their applause audibly distinct. The video concluded with a close-up of a smiling young boy in a blue and black kit, with the Dubai skyline in the background, as Davies inquired, 'Amazing. What does that mean when you see that?'

event_blocks.summary: Amanda Davies, elegantly attired in a sparkling black gown and diamond necklace, interviewed Erling Haaland, who wore a bright blue double-breasted suit and white bow tie, his blonde hair styled in a bun. Haaland reflected on his career, stating, "no matter where you come from... you can come far," attributing his success to hard work and supportive people. As the audience applauded from their white armchairs, Davies congratulated Haaland, who then departed the stage. The scene shifted to a wide shot of the stage, featuring the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBE SOCCER AWARDS' logo against a dark backdrop with pulsing orange and yellow geometric lights. Tom Urquhart, in a black tuxedo, stood at a golden circular podium, welcoming Haaland and remarking on the "magical setting of the Atlantis the Palm." Amanda Davies then appeared at a second golden podium, holding a blue folder, announcing the "14th edition of the Globe Soccer Awards" and inviting all winners to join Haaland for a "truly magical evening." Urquhart invited the audience to "sit back" amidst dynamic golden light patterns and uplifting awards music. Later, Urquhart encouraged applause for "the star performers of 2023," while Davies announced, "A huge congratulations to the simply incredible Cristiano Ronaldo, please come and join us." Urquhart then invited "the entire Manchester City team, backroom staff, all the winners," and Davies added, "Including their coach, coach of the year, Pep Guardiola," to further applause. The grand Event Venue Interior, with its audience in white armchairs, showcased the two golden podiums and the event logo. Urquhart continued by inviting "career award winners as well, of course, John Terry," followed by "Lionel Scaloni," and "the Brazilian Manchester United great Casemiro," to the stage, eliciting more applause. Dignitaries, including a Recipient in White Thobe and a Recipient in Green Thobe, flanked the stage. Davies expressed gratitude to "His Highness Sheikh Mansour bin Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum, Chairman of the Dubai Sports Council, and His Excellency Mattar Al Tayer, Vice Chairman of the Dubai Sports Council for gracing us with their presence." Men in suits, including Kyle Walker and Cristiano Ronaldo, were observed walking through audience aisles, some holding Golden Trophies. Close-ups captured Marcel Koller's engagement and Pep Guardiola's thoughtful clapping. The event culminated with multiple award winners, including Erling Haaland, gathering on stage with their trophies alongside dignitaries and hosts, under the glowing Dubai Globe Soccer Awards logo, as Tom began to call on more individuals amidst the celebratory atmosphere and dynamic lighting. The ceremony concluded with a group of formally dressed male footballers, including Erling Haaland and Cristiano Ronaldo, standing on stage, each holding a golden, globe-shaped trophy. A male host thanked attendees and invited "George" to "come and join the party." The camera panned across the winners as the host thanked partners, sponsors, and the audience. The scene pulled back to reveal the grand stage with golden railings and a dark, starry backdrop illuminated by pulsing orange and yellow geometric light patterns, with an audience seated in white armchairs. Amanda Davies stood at a golden podium alongside a man in a white thobe. The host addressed Davies, who responded, "Thank you." The host then thanked those tuning in, expressing hope to see them next year in Dubai or Sardinia. As the camera focused back on the group of winners walking forward, shaking hands and exchanging brief words, the host concluded the ceremony, stating, "From all of us here in Dubai, it is... Good night!" Amanda Davies echoed "Good night!" amidst applause and upbeat music, followed by a dynamic animated graphic of the event title: 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS'.
